mnemonics.title: Circular Queue Position Calculation
mnemonics.content: To remember next position formula: NEXT = (CURRENT + 1) % SIZE - Think of it as 'Moving in a circle, modulo brings you back' - The % operator makes it circular.

mnemonics.title: Queue Full vs Empty Detection
mnemonics.content: For circular queue: EMPTY = front == rear; FULL = (rear + 1) % MAX == front - Remember: 'One space stays empty in full queue to distinguish from empty'.

mnemonics.title: Stack Push-Pop Operations
mnemonics.content: PUSH: Top++, then A[Top] = x; POP: x = A[Top], then Top-- - Think: 'Push up, pop down' - Stack grows upward, shrinks downward.

acronyms.usage: Principle followed by queue data structure - the first element inserted is the first one to be removed.
Principle followed by stack data structure - the last element inserted is the first one to be removed.
Queue allowing insertion and deletion at both ends.
Position where elements are added in a queue.
Position from where elements are removed in a queue.

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a clock face - after 12 (last position), it wraps to 1 (first position). The front and rear pointers move clockwise, and when they meet, the queue is either full or empty.

Think of a partitioned storage room where each stack has its designated section. When one stack needs more space, it can only take space from its designated boundary, not from others (in fixed partition method).
Picture a circular rotating door - when the trailing edge catches up to the leading edge, there's no more room to enter, even though there's space behind where people left.

analogies.analogy: Like multiple elevators in a building sharing the same shaft but stopping at different floor ranges - each elevator (stack) has its designated floors but they share the same physical space.
Think of a round dinner table where guests (elements) are seated. When a guest leaves (dequeue), their chair becomes available for a new guest (enqueue) regardless of where they sit.
Imagine a parking lot with multiple rows but a shared entrance - even if other rows have empty spaces, if the row for a specific vehicle type is full, no more vehicles of that type can enter.
